mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and improved liquidity conditions, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional baseline when FEDFUNDS is falling or below 0.25%, or if the sign flips negative in easing windows over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and slower demand, which leads analysts to cut earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations generate positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after a 3-month UNRATE rise is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS together with rising HOUST indicates broad household and retail demand strength, which supports revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint high-RSAFS and high-HOUST periods do not outperform baseline over 60 trading days, or if either series alone explains the effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL implies stronger real output growth without an inflation shock, which eases margin pressure and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation over 3 months stays below 2% annualized while INDPRO rises
failure_condition: Rejected if industrial production gains paired with low CPI inflation do not produce positive excess 20-day returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant across all post-2005 samples.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in HOUST signals housing-led demand recovery and improved cyclical sentiment, which tends to precede higher equity prices over the next month as investors reprice growth-sensitive sectors.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Rejected if HOUST breakouts fail to outperform unconditional 20-day returns, or if the signal becomes negative during more than half of breakout episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies genuine labor-market deterioration, which compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if days with falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE do not have negative average 5-day returns, or if the sign is positive in most recession-scare observations.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL is not surging, liquidity is abundant and real discount rates stay compressed, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS stays below 0.50% while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation remains below 3%
failure_condition: Rejected if zero-rate, low-inflation windows fail to outperform baseline over 60 trading days, or if higher returns are not concentrated in the low-rate/low-inflation joint state.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDPC1 growth combined with soft RSAFS indicates slowing final demand, which should lead to downward revisions in corporate revenue expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth is negative and RSAFS growth is below zero
failure_condition: Rejected if joint GDP and retail sales weakness does not precede negative 60-day forward returns, or if the observed return distribution is indistinguishable from baseline across slowdown episodes.
